Title: Thoughts on Role Confusion

Title: The End of the Craftsman?
Description: I noticed something a few months ago: I was talking to my colleagues less. Nothing was wrong. I’d have a question, describe it to an AI, get something useful back. Why pull in a human when the loop already closed? It took me a while to notice what that was costing.

Title: Reinventing The Wheel, Now At A Bargain Price! – zwischenzugs.com
Description: For twenty years, saying “don’t reinvent the wheel” was usually enough to win any argument about writing bespoke software. But that advice assumed two things: writing code was expensive, and importing code was cheap. AI and supply-chain governance are changing both assumptions.

Title: Framework's 10G Ethernet module exposes USB-C's complexity
Description:
I've been following WisdPi's development of various 5 Gbps and 10 Gbps Ethernet adapters for the past couple years.
They use newer Realtek Ethernet chips, which sometimes have performance quirks—most frequently encountered under Linux.
In today's video, I tested the new WisdPi 10G Ethernet Expansion Card for Framework computers. It fits in any available Framework Expansion slot—even on the Framework Desktop.
But Expansion Cards use USB-C for their connection to the mainboard—and therein lies the rub...
